content.question: To what extent would legal abolition or phase-out of the 6- and 8-year tracks reduce stratification in the broader Hungarian secondary system, versus triggering formation of de facto parallel elite tracks through private schools, church-maintained alternatives, or informal tutoring-based selection?
content.reduction_path: Model counterfactual scenarios using international case studies (e.g., Germany's Gymnasium abolition attempts, Czech Republic's track persistence despite reform efforts) and survey displaced parents and school operators on alternative institutional choices.
